task:
Atom 1 of Ticket 0067 (migrate legacy flat local/runs/<runId> dirs to ADR-0027 §6 nested local/runs/<workspaceId>/<runId>). This atom is the PURE, TESTED core function only — do NOT wire any caller, daemon, CLI, or perform a real move yet.

WHERE: add the function next to its layout owner in packages/core/src/runner/run-dir.ts (the same file that owns localRunDir/resolveLocalRunDir — keep one owner for the layout). Export it from the package barrels that already export localRunDir/resolveLocalRunDir (packages/core/src/runner/index.ts and packages/core/src/index.ts). Add unit tests to packages/core/tests/run-dir.test.ts.

WHAT TO ADD:
  export interface FlatRunDirMigrationReport {
    moved: Array<{ runId: string; from: string; to: string }>
    skippedActive: string[]
    skippedUnknownWorkspace: string[]
    skippedTargetExists: string[]
  }
  export function migrateLegacyFlatRunDirs(
    runsRoot: string,
    resolveWorkspaceId: (runId: string) => string | null,
    isActive: (runId: string) => boolean,
  ): FlatRunDirMigrationReport

BEHAVIOR (synchronous, node:fs):
  1. readdirSync(runsRoot, {withFileTypes:true}); if runsRoot is unreadable/missing, return an all-empty report (do not throw).
  2. For each TOP-LEVEL directory entry whose name is treated as a candidate runId:
     - Use resolveWorkspaceId(entry.name) as the SOLE discriminator of 'is this a legacy flat run dir'. If it returns null, the entry is NOT a known run (e.g. it is a workspace namespace dir like 'cocoder', or an unknown dir) -> record in skippedUnknownWorkspace and leave it COMPLETELY untouched. (Do not move, do not recurse.)
     - If resolveWorkspaceId returns a workspaceId and isActive(runId) is true -> skippedActive, leave untouched.
     - Otherwise target = join(runsRoot, workspaceId, runId). If target already exists -> skippedTargetExists, leave the flat dir untouched (NEVER clobber/merge an existing nested dir). Else mkdirSync(join(runsRoot, workspaceId), {recursive:true}) and renameSync(flat, target); push {runId, from, to} to moved.
  3. IDEMPOTENT: a second run finds no flat dirs left for already-moved runs (they now live nested and are not top-level run candidates because the discriminator on the now-empty flat path won't be hit — flat is gone), so it returns empty moved. Running twice must be a no-op the second time. Make the loop tolerant of entries that are files (skip non-directories).

ACCEPTANCE / EXIT CRITERION (scripted): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test run-dir` (or the repo's vitest invocation for packages/core/tests/run-dir.test.ts) passes, and `pnpm -w typecheck` (or the repo typecheck) is green. Tests MUST cover: (a) a flat dir with a known active workspaceId is moved to nested and contents preserved; (b) skippedActive when isActive returns true; (c) skippedUnknownWorkspace for a dir the map doesn't know (e.g. a workspace namespace dir 'cocoder' that itself contains a nested run) and that dir is left byte-identical; (d) skippedTargetExists when the nested target already exists (flat left untouched, nested NOT clobbered); (e) idempotency: calling migrateLegacyFlatRunDirs twice yields empty moved on the second call. Use mkdtemp throwaway roots exactly like the existing resolveLocalRunDir test.

MUST NOT BREAK: existing localRunDir/resolveLocalRunDir behavior and their existing tests; do not change resolveLocalRunDir (the compat read-fallback stays — its retirement is out of scope for this atom). Do not touch the daemon, CLI, or any real local/runs data. Keep the function pure of liveness/store knowledge — liveness comes only through the isActive predicate and the runId->workspaceId mapping only through resolveWorkspaceId, so the caller supplies authoritative sources later.

Write scope: packages/core/src/runner/run-dir.ts, packages/core/src/runner/index.ts, packages/core/src/index.ts, packages/core/tests/run-dir.test.ts.

Atom 2 of Ticket 0067 — wire the (already-landed, unit-tested) core function migrateLegacyFlatRunDirs into the daemon so the one-time physical move of legacy flat local/runs/<runId> dirs into local/runs/<workspaceId>/<runId> happens automatically and idempotently at daemon startup, at the proven-safe point where no run is live.

WHERE & DESIGN:
  1. In packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts add a new exported function `migrateLegacyRunDirsOnce(ctx: OzContext): void` as a SIBLING to `reconcileOrphans` (do NOT fold it into reconcileOrphans — reconcileOrphans owns DB status; this owns dir layout; one responsibility each). It must:
     - Build the runId->workspaceId map from `ctx.store.listRuns()` (no filter = all runs): const wsById = new Map(ctx.store.listRuns().map((r) => [r.id, r.workspaceId])).
     - Build authoritative liveness from `ctx.inFlight` ONLY (ADR-0027: live state is OzContext.inFlight, NEVER DB status): const liveRunIds = new Set(ctx.inFlight.values()).
     - Call the core helper: const report = migrateLegacyFlatRunDirs(ctx.runsRoot, (runId) => wsById.get(runId) ?? null, (runId) => liveRunIds.has(runId)) — import migrateLegacyFlatRunDirs from '@cocoder/core'.
     - For observability, for each entry in report.moved record a run-scoped event: ctx.store.recordEvent({ runId: entry.runId, type: 'run-dir-migrated', data: { from: entry.from, to: entry.to } }). Do not record events for skipped entries.
  2. In packages/daemon/src/server.ts call it ONCE at startup IMMEDIATELY AFTER `await reconcileOrphans(ctx)` (the existing comment there already establishes 'the live set is empty here' — that is exactly the safe window). Add a one-line comment tying it to ADR-0027 §6 step 5.

WHY THIS POINT IS SAFE (must remain true): at boot ctx.inFlight is a fresh empty Map and no run process exists yet, so no dir is being written; reconcileOrphans has just flipped any stale 'running' DB rows to 'failed'. liveRunIds is therefore empty and every known flat dir migrates; unknown dirs (e.g. the 'cocoder' workspace namespace dir) and any future in-flight run are skipped by the core function's own logic.

MUST NOT BREAK / CONSTRAINTS:
  - Do NOT change the core migrateLegacyFlatRunDirs signature or behavior (atom 1, already committed) and do NOT change reconcileOrphans behavior.
  - Do NOT read DB run.status as liveness — liveness is ctx.inFlight only.
  - Do NOT move dirs anywhere except via the core helper; no second copy of the move logic.
  - Keep it synchronous and best-effort: the core helper already swallows an unreadable runsRoot by returning an empty report; do not let migration throw and abort daemon boot (if you are unsure any store/recordEvent call can throw, guard the whole body so a migration failure never prevents the server from starting, and is the only thing that can be skipped).

ACCEPTANCE / EXIT CRITERION (scripted): add a daemon test (extend packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts near the existing reconcileOrphans coverage, or a new packages/daemon/tests/run-dir-migration.test.ts) using the existing in-memory store harness (openRunStore(':memory:')) and a mkdtemp runsRoot, that proves: (a) given a committed run (id run_x, workspaceId cocoder) and a flat dir <runsRoot>/run_x with a file inside, after calling migrateLegacyRunDirsOnce(ctx) the dir is at <runsRoot>/cocoder/run_x with the file preserved and the flat path gone, and a 'run-dir-migrated' event was recorded for run_x; (b) when ctx.inFlight has that run (set ctx.inFlight.set('cocoder', 'run_x')), the flat dir is left untouched and no event recorded. The new test plus `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec tsc --noEmit` (daemon typecheck) and the daemon test suite must be green.

Write scope: packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, packages/daemon/src/server.ts, packages/daemon/tests/** (the test file you add/extend).

Atom 3 of Ticket 0067 — add a runnable end-to-end proof harness that boots the REAL daemon (createOzServer) and proves the ADR-0027 §6 step-5 legacy-flat run-dir migration actually runs on startup. This closes the gap between the unit/integration tests (which call the function directly) and the real boot path (server.ts calls migrateLegacyRunDirsOnce after reconcileOrphans).

WHERE: create scripts/proof-run-dir-migration.mjs, modeled CLOSELY on the existing scripts/proof-workspace-segmentation.mjs (reuse its conventions verbatim: the `probe` string run via `pnpm exec tsx --eval`, a MARKER line carrying a JSON model, the fakeGit()/fakeHost()/okAdapter/controlledIO()/call() helpers, mkdtemp throwaway install root, and the loadModel()+assert()+fail() verifier with a per-objective evidence print). Register it in package.json scripts as "proof:run-dir-migration": "node scripts/proof-run-dir-migration.mjs" (keep the existing alphabetical-ish ordering near the other proof: entries).

WHAT THE PROOF MUST DEMONSTRATE (positive run):
  1. Set up a throwaway install home: <install>/local. Open the store at <install>/local/proof.db (openRunStore with an injected monotonic now like the segmentation harness). upsertWorkspace({id:'cocoder', path, name}). Create a completed run: const run = store.createRun({workspaceId:'cocoder', priorityId:'demo'}); store.setRunStatus(run.id,'completed').
  2. Seed a LEGACY FLAT run dir at <install>/local/runs/<run.id>/ containing a file (e.g. state.json with known contents) BEFORE booting the server.
  3. Boot the real daemon: oz = await createOzServer({ cocoderHome: install, port: 0, store, git: fakeGit(), sessionHost: fakeHost(), getAdapter: () => okAdapter, io: controlledIO().io, runHeadless: async () => ({exitCode:0, output:''}), restartDaemon: () => {} }). createOzServer runs reconcileOrphans then migrateLegacyRunDirsOnce at startup — do NOT call the migration function directly; the whole point is to prove the boot wiring.
  4. After boot, assert via the model: <install>/local/runs/cocoder/<run.id>/state.json exists with the original contents; the flat path <install>/local/runs/<run.id>/state.json no longer exists; and store.listEvents(run.id) contains a 'run-dir-migrated' event whose data.from/data.to match the flat and nested paths. Close oz and rm the temp root in finally.

NEGATIVE SELF-CHECK (mandatory, mirror the PROOF_*_INJECT_REGRESSION convention in the segmentation harness header): support an env var PROOF_RUN_DIR_MIGRATION_INJECT_REGRESSION that injects a fault which the migration must catch, so the verifier reports FAIL (process exit non-zero) when set. Implement the injection as: when the var is set (e.g. value 'unregistered'), SKIP registering the run in the store (so resolveWorkspaceId returns null and the flat dir is correctly NOT migrated) while the assertions still demand the nested dir — i.e. the proof's own assertions must fail, demonstrating the check has teeth. The verifier must exit 0 in the normal run and non-zero in the injected run.

ACCEPTANCE / EXIT CRITERION (scripted, run BOTH):
  - `node scripts/proof-run-dir-migration.mjs` exits 0 and prints a clear objective->evidence map (flat→nested move + event recorded).
  - `PROOF_RUN_DIR_MIGRATION_INJECT_REGRESSION=unregistered node scripts/proof-run-dir-migration.mjs` exits NON-zero (the self-check catches the injected regression).

MUST NOT BREAK / CONSTRAINTS: do not modify any product code (packages/**) — this atom is the harness only; if you find the boot path does not actually migrate, STOP and report it rather than editing product code under this directive. Use only throwaway mkdtemp roots; never touch the real local/runs data. Keep the harness self-contained and dependency-free beyond what the segmentation harness already uses (node:fs/promises, node:http, node:os, node:path, tsx, @cocoder/core, ./packages/daemon/src/index.ts).

Write scope: scripts/proof-run-dir-migration.mjs, package.json (scripts section only).
